description: Brix Money / iTRY token system — 15 contracts, 1324 nSLOC

findings.title: User can bypass staking restrictions through composer and deposit on another chain
findings.description: SOFT_RESTRICTED_STAKER_ROLE check is on the composer address (address(this)), not the actual depositor. Restricted users route through wiTryOFTAdapter → LayerZero → wiTryVaultComposer to bypass restrictions. Vulnerable code in VaultComposerSync library, inherited by in-scope wiTryVaultComposer.

findings.title: Cross-chain unstake and fast redeem fail due to LayerZero dust removal
findings.description: minAmountLD set equal to amountLD. LayerZero _removeDust truncates amounts not divisible by 1e12, causing SlippageExceeded revert. Affects ~69% of real-world amounts. Also in _fastRedeem at line 116-117.

findings.title: Incompatibility with AA wallets and multisigs due to enforced address symmetry in cross chain unstaking
findings.description: user field hardcoded to msg.sender. On hub chain, assets sent to same address. Multisigs/AA wallets with different addresses across chains permanently lose assets.

findings.title: unstakeThroughComposer lacks cooldownDuration == 0 bypass causing inconsistent behavior for cross-chain users when admin disables cooldown
findings.description: Missing '|| cooldownDuration == 0' check present in base unstake(). When admin disables cooldown, cross-chain users still wait up to 90 days while local users claim immediately.

findings.title: Missing access control in FastAccessVault.rebalanceFunds() enables griefing attacks
findings.description: Function is external without onlyOwner modifier despite interface specifying 'only callable by owner'. Anyone can trigger, enabling liquidity griefing by repeatedly forcing excess back to custodian.

findings.title: FULL_RESTRICTED_STAKER_ROLE users can bypass access controls via composer-created cooldowns
findings.description: _withdraw validates composer (caller/receiver/owner) not redeemer. Fully restricted users get cooldown entitlements and later call unstake() which has no role checks.

findings.title: burnExcessITry() burns from admin's balance instead of target address
findings.description: _burn(msg.sender, iTRYAmount) requires admin to hold the tokens. No parameter to specify a different address — two transactions needed instead of one for emergency burns.

findings.title: Blacklist mechanism enables owner to confiscate user funds via redistributeLockedAmount()
findings.description: Admin can blacklist any user then mint their entire wiTRY balance to arbitrary address. Goes beyond freeze — enables full confiscation without recourse.

findings.title: YieldForwarder.rescueToken() lacks protection against rescuing yieldToken, enabling owner to divert yield
findings.description: No check for token == address(yieldToken). Owner can divert yield funds. Inconsistent with StakediTry.rescueTokens which explicitly blocks rescuing the asset.
